item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents strongly reject society-over-individual throughout. Agent A visibly hardens slightly by turn 5, describing common-good reasoning as a potential excuse for pressure, conformity, and abuse of power. Agent B remains steady from turn 2 to turn 6. Both concede only narrow, accountable limits in emergencies or clear-harm cases, not the core claim.
